_doc: Coverage thresholds enforced by tools/coverage/check-thresholds.ps1. Default applies to every class. Per-class overrides MUST include a 'reason' string; the check script prints these in the failure report so reviewers can audit why a class is exempt. NEVER add an entry without a reason — that defeats the purpose of the gate.

MatPlotLibNet.Rendering.Svg.Svg3DRotatio.reason: Pure JS string template — no testable C# branches.

MatPlotLibNet.Rendering.Svg.SvgTreemapDr.reason: JS-string template — no testable C# branches.

MatPlotLibNet.Rendering.SeriesRenderers..reason: Step interpolation has multiple symmetric branches per mode; 80% branch is achievable, 90% requires testing internal-equivalent code paths.

MatPlotLibNet.Indicators.Adx.reason: Remaining 4 unhit branches are provably-unreachable defensive code: line 82 'if (resultLen <= 0)' is guarded by line 58 'if (n <= _period * 2)' so resultLen is always > 0 when reached; lines 93/94/96 'smoothTr > 0 ? : 0' and 'diSum > 0 ? : 0' ternaries cannot return 0 because reaching them already requires non-degenerate price movement (n > 2*period bars).

MatPlotLibNet.Playground.Pages.Playgroun.reason: Blazor page — UI event wiring only; all testable logic extracted to PlaygroundController (tested at 100%). Blazor-specific @code branches (MarkupString toggle, async JS interop) are not exercisable without a full browser runtime.

MatPlotLibNet.Playground.Layout.MainLayo.reason: Sample Blazor layout — interactive demo, not library code.

MatPlotLibNet.Playground.PlaygroundExamp.reason: Sample switch over PlaygroundExample enum. Default (unknown-value) arm is tested via Build_UnregisteredEnumValue_ThrowsArgumentException. Branch threshold raised to 90 after HasCartesianSpines and Build exception tests were added (Phase K).

MatPlotLibNet.Playground.PlaygroundOptio.reason: Sample Blazor state record — visual property setters not exercised by automated tests.

Program.reason: Sample console entry point — not library code.

MatPlotLibNet.Indicators.Streaming.IStre.reason: Interface declaration; concrete StreamingCci/Obv/Vwap/etc tests cover the contract.

MatPlotLibNet.Rendering.ISeriesVisitor.reason: Interface with 14 default no-op `Visit(...) { }` overloads (ISeriesVisitor.cs lines 195-243) for ISP compatibility — every concrete visitor (SvgSeriesVisitor, TestSeriesVisitor, etc.) overrides every method, so the default empty bodies are never reached at runtime by design. Same pattern as the existing IStreamingIndicator exemption. Concrete visitor implementations are tested via SvgSeriesRenderer + AllSeriesTests Theory. Phase Y.1 (v1.7.2, 2026-04-19).

MatPlotLibNet.Indicators.PriceIndicator<.reason: Generic abstract base; the unmeasured 'line' is an empty body inside a single instantiation that disappears under JIT specialisation. Branch is already 100. Concrete subclasses (Sma/Ema/Rsi/etc.) cover all reachable production paths. Same shape as the existing IStreamingIndicator exemption. Phase Z.9 (v1.7.2, 2026-04-19).

MatPlotLibNet.Playground.PlaygroundExamp.reason: Sample-only helper. DisplayName fallback (no Description attribute → ToString) and FromDisplayName null-return arm are both tested via PlaygroundExampleEnumTests (Phase K). Thresholds raised to 90/90.

MatPlotLibNet.Interaction.DataCursorEven.reason: Sealed record — auto-generated equality/Deconstruct have no testable branches in the C# project.

MatPlotLibNet.Interaction.HoverTooltipCo.reason: Sealed record — auto-generated equality has no testable branches.

MatPlotLibNet.Interaction.PinnedAnnotati.reason: Sealed record — auto-generated equality has no testable branches.

MatPlotLibNet.Skia.SkiaGlyphPathProvider.reason: Skia glyph-to-path provider — registered but not exercised by tests; runtime code path used only by SkiaSharp text-rendering pipeline. Tracked for follow-up coverage.

MatPlotLibNet.Rendering.HatchRenderer.reason: Pattern hatching — only invoked when series.Hatch is set, no series in current cookbook uses it. Tracked for follow-up coverage.

MatPlotLibNet.Interactive.BrowserLaunche.reason: Calls Process.Start with UseShellExecute=true to invoke the OS browser launcher (xdg-open / open / explorer). Cannot unit-test without mocking System.Diagnostics.Process, which would only test the mock and not the launch contract — observed at runtime on every CI run as 'xdg-open: no method available for opening …' from MatPlotLibNet.Interactive.Tests.InteractiveExtensionsTests.ShowAsync_OpensBrowser (already SKIPped on CI: 'Requires a desktop environment'). Phase X.0 (v1.7.2, 2026-04-19).
